section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: In most reactor environments the helium is generated from the transmutation of $^{10}\mathrm{B}$ .

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: Boron is generally present in nickel-base alloys at concentrations in the range of 5 to 80 ppm by weight.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: The Stellarite Division of Union Carbide has melted a 75-lb heat of Hastelloy N, using a practice designed to produce low residuals of boron, oxygen, nitrogen, hydrogen, silicon, and sulfur.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: This vacuum-induction heat, designated heat 65-552, was melted using an alumina crucible.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: These data are given in Table 1.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: The concentration of boron is much less than the normal 5 to 80 ppm.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: It is apparent that most of the boron was not introduced into the alloy from the raw materials listed in Table 1.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: Approximately $84\%$ of the boron in the ingot was introduced by some other means.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: It has been demonstrated that a heat of Hastelloy N can be produced in 50 to 75-lb ingot sizes that contain substantially lower quantities of boron than is normally found in these grades of material.

section: PRODUCTION OF A LOW-BORON HEAT OF HASTELLOY N
text: It therefore seems probable that larger ingots in the range of 10,000 lb can also be produced which could offer improved properties for reactor application at elevated temperatures.
